problem:
The transformation $T,$ taking vectors to vectors, has the following properties:

(i) $T(a \mathbf{v} + b \mathbf{w}) = a T(\mathbf{v}) + b T(\mathbf{w})$ for all vectors $\mathbf{v}$ and $\mathbf{w},$ and for all scalars $a$ and $b.$
(ii) $T(\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{w}) = T(\mathbf{v}) \times T(\mathbf{w})$ for all vectors $\mathbf{v}$ and $\mathbf{w}.$
(iii) $T \begin{pmatrix} 6 \\ 6 \\ 3 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 4 \\ -1 \\ 8 \end{pmatrix}.$
(iv) $T \begin{pmatrix} -6 \\ 3 \\ 6 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 4 \\ 8 \\ -1 \end{pmatrix}.$

Find $T \begin{pmatrix} 3 \\ 9 \\ 12 \end{pmatrix}.$

classified_questions.question: What are the properties of transformation T mentioned in the problem statement?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can you explain what the cross product is and how property (ii) relates to it?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Using property (iii) and (iv), can you find the vector T(27, -54, 54)?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we use the linearity property (i) to find T(3, 9, 12)?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Using the linear combination found in the solution, how can we apply property (i) to find T(3, 9, 12)?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final result of T(3, 9, 12)?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you provide additional example(s) of linear transformations that satisfy the properties (i)-(iv) discussed in this problem?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: What is the significance of the matrix found in the solution, and how does it relate to T as a rotation in space?
classified_questions.label: e
